author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, John ii. 2, ‘and Jesus also was bidden, and his disciples, to the marriage.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John ii. 2, ‘and Jesus also was bidden, and his disciples, to the marriage.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye know, Brethren, for ye have learnt it as believing in Christ, and continually too do we by our ministry impress it upon you, that the humility of Christ is the medicine of man’s swollen pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For man would not have perished, had he not been swollen up through pride.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For “pride,” as saith the Scripture, “is the beginning of all sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Against the beginning of sin, the beginning of righteousness was necessary.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If then pride be the beginning of all sin, whereby should the swelling of pride be cured, had not God vouchsafed to humble Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let man blush to be proud, seeing that God hath humbled Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For when man is told to humble himself, he disdains it; and when men are injured, it is pride that makes them wish to be avenged.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Forasmuch as they disdain to humble themselves, they wish to be avenged; as if another’s punishment could be any profit to any man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One who has been hurt and suffered wrong wishes to be avenged; he seeks his own remedy from another’s punishment, and gains a great torment.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Christ therefore vouchsafed to humble Himself in all things, showing us the way; if we but think meet to walk thereby.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Among His other acts, lo, the Virgin’s Son comes to the marriage; who being with the Father instituted marriage.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As the first woman, by whom came sin, was made of a man without a woman; so the Man by whom sin was done away, was made of a woman without a man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the first we fell, by the other we rise.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what did He at this marriage?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of water He made wine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What greater sign of power?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who had power to do such things, vouchsafed to be in need.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who made of water wine could also have of stones made bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The power was the same; but then the devil tempted Him, therefore Christ did it not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye know that when the Lord Christ was tempted, the devil suggested this to Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He was an hungred, since this too He vouchsafed to be, since this too made part of His Humiliation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Bread was hungry, as the Way fainted, as saving Health was wounded, as the Life died.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When then He was an hungred as ye know, the tempter said to Him, “If Thou be the Son of God, command that these stones be made bread.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He made answer to the tempter, teaching thee to answer the tempter.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For to this end does the general fight, that the soldiers may learn.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What answer did He make?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Man doth not live by bread alone, but by every word of God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He did not make bread of the stones, who of course could as easily have done it, as He made of water wine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it is an exercise of the same power to make bread of stone; but He did it not, that He might despise the tempter’s will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For no otherwise is the tempter overcome, but by being despised.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And when He had overcome the devil’s temptation, “Angels came and ministered to Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who had so great power, why did He not do the one, and do the other?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Read, yea, recollect what thou hast just heard, when He did this, when, that is, He made of the water wine; what did the Evangelist add?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And His disciples believed on Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would the devil on the other occasion have believed on Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who could do so great things, was hungry, and athirst, was wearied, slept, was apprehended, beaten, crucified, slain.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the way; walk by humility, that thou mayest come to eternity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ-God is the Country whither we go; Christ-Man is the Way whereby we go.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To Him we go, by Him we go; why fear we lest we go astray?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He departed not from the Father; and came to us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He sucked the breasts, and He contained the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He lay in the manger, and He fed the Angels.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God and Man, the same God who is Man, the same Man who is God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But not God in that wherein He is Man, God, in that He is the Word; Man, in that the Word was made Flesh; by at once continuing to be God, and by assuming man’s Flesh; by adding what He was not, not losing what He was.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore henceforward, having now suffered in this His humiliation, dead, and buried, He has now risen again, and ascended into heaven, there He is, and sitteth at the right Hand of the Father: and here He is needy in His poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yesterday too I set this forth to your Affection by occasion of what He said to Nathanael, “Thou shalt see a greater thing than this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For I say unto you, Ye shall see Heaven open, and the Angels of God ascending and descending unto the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We searched out what this meant, and spake at some length; must we recapitulate the same to-day?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let those who were present remember; yet I will briefly run over it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would not say, “ascending unto the Son of Man,” unless He were above; He would not say, “descending unto the Son of Man,” unless He were also below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is at once above, and below; above in Himself, below in His; above with the Father, below in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Whence also was that Voice to Saul, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He would not say, “Saul, Saul,” unless that He was above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But Saul was not persecuting Him above.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who was above would not have said, “Why persecutest thou me?” unless He were below also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fear Christ above; recognise Him below.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have Christ above bestowing His bounty, recognise Him here in need.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Here He is poor, there He is rich.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That Christ is poor here, He tells us Himself for me, “I was an hungred, I was thirsty, I was naked, I was a stranger, I was in prison.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And to some He said, “Ye have ministered unto Me,” and to some He said, “Ye have not ministered unto Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, we have proved Christ poor; that Christ is Rich, who knows not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And even here it was a property of these riches to turn the water into wine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he who has wine is rich, how rich is He who maketh wine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then Christ is rich and poor; as God, rich; as Man, poor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea rich too now as Very Man He hath ascended into heaven, sitteth at the right Hand of the Father; yet still He is poor and hungry here, thirsty, and naked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What art thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Rich, or poor?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Many tell me, I am poor; and they tell the truth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I recognise some poor having something, and some having want.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But some have much gold and silver.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: O that they would acknowledge themselves poor!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Poor they will acknowledge themselves, if they acknowledge the poor about them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how is it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much soever thou hast, thou rich man whosoever thou art, thou art God’s beggar.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The hour of prayer comes, and there I prove thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou makest thy petition.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How art thou not poor, who makest thy petition?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say more, Thou makest petition for bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wilt thou not have to say, “Give us our daily bread”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou, who askest for daily bread, art thou poor, or rich?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And yet Christ saith to thee, “Give Me of that which I have given thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what didst thou bring here, when thou camest hither?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: All things that I created, thyself created hast found here; nothing didst thou bring, nothing shalt thou take away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why wilt thou not give Me of Mine Own?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For thou art full, and the poor man is empty.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Look at your first origin; naked were ye both born.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou too then wast born naked.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Great store hast thou found here; didst thou bring ought with thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask for Mine Own; give, and I will repay.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou hast found Me a bountiful giver, make Me at once thy debtor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not enough to say, ‘Thou hast found Me a bountiful giver, make Me at once thy debtor;’ let Me regard thee as lending upon interest.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou givest me but little, I will repay more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou givest me earthly things, I will repay heavenly.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou givest me temporal things, I will restore eternal.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I will restore thee to thyself, when I shall have restored thee unto Me.”
